steps.title: Margin of error defines a confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error tells us the range around the sample mean where the true population mean is expected to fall.

We construct this interval by adding and subtracting the margin of error from the sample mean:
$$\text{Confidence interval} = [\text{Sample mean} - \text{Margin of error}, \text{Sample mean} + \text{Margin of error}]$$

steps.title: Calculate the interval bounds
steps.content:
Substitute the given values:
$$\text{Lower bound} = 2.75 - 0.08 = 2.67$$
$$\text{Upper bound} = 2.75 + 0.08 = 2.83$$
The confidence interval is from 2.67 dollars to 2.83 dollars.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• The interval from 2.67 dollars to 2.83 dollars: This is exactly the confidence interval — a plausible range for the true population mean. ✓
  • The outside-interval option: This asserts the true mean falls outside the confidence interval, which is implausible. ✗
  • The below-2.67-dollars option: Only the far lower tail — not supported by the data. ✗
  • The above-2.83-dollars option: Only the far upper tail — not supported by the data. ✗
That matches Choice A — It is between \$2.67 and \$2.83.
